title: Dividing then multiplying back by (x2 - x1) does not always recover (y2 - y1)
informal_statement: It is not the case that for all reals x1, y1, x2, y2: (y2 - y1) / (x2 - x1) * (x2 - x1) = y2 - y1. The identity only holds when x2 != x1; witnessed by x1 = x2 = 0, y1 = 0, y2 = 5, where the left side collapses to 0 (division by zero is 0 in Lean's Field) while the right side is 5.
informal_proof_idea: Assume the universal claim for contradiction, instantiate at x1=0, y1=0, x2=0, y2=5, and let norm_num compute (5 - 0) / (0 - 0) * (0 - 0) = 0 versus 5 - 0 = 5, closing the goal by the resulting numeral contradiction.
formal_statement_pp: theorem slope_div_mul_not_always_cancel : ¬ (∀ x1 y1 x2 y2 : ℝ, (y2 - y1) / (x2 - x1) * (x2 - x1) = y2 - y1)

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 9d78c78c, problem_version 4581a48a-8214-42e8-83e7-6b458eaa8f6a). Companion to the negative example negative.geometry.slope_div_mul.division_by_zero_unguarded.v1, which captures the natural-but-false direct attempt on the unconditional claim (field_simp, leaving an unsolved_goals residual) in a separate tracked episode (2409c443).
